finding: Sanitizing one provider response is insufficient if retained evidence artifacts are not independently scanned before upload. A fail-closed recursive privacy gate should reject secret-bearing keys, provider message fields, malformed JSON, and secret-shaped string values while permitting bounded provenance, deployment URLs, stable identifiers, and numeric diagnostic codes.

cases: accept bounded provenance and HTTPS deployment URLs

uncertainty: The validator is not yet wired into the production workflow before artifact upload, and it has not been executed against a live workflow evidence bundle.
falsification_route: Construct a retained JSON artifact containing a forbidden nested key or secret-shaped value and verify the command exits non-zero; then run the validator against every exact upload-path artifact in GitHub Actions before upload-artifact.
epistemic_limit: Privacy-gate acceptance only establishes that the inspected JSON avoids the encoded disclosure patterns. It does not prove deployment success, source truth, scientific validity, or absence of every possible secret format.
